issue: has_key() undefined at runtime despite grammar
workaround: awk pre-join OR parallel [keys, values] lists
xs.push(v) for O(1) — 42s to 0.3s on 50K entries

issue: pure fn + struct return causes OOB (index 2 out of bounds len 2)

workaround: env var fallback OR argv resolver

issue: R19 silent catch lint blocks intentional fallback
composite slice expr a[k+nl..line.len()] returns wrong region
workaround: bind start/end to lets first: let s0=k+nl; let ll=line.len(); a[s0..ll]

issue: verdict string extraction off-by-one — index_of("\"verdict\"")+11 lands past closing-quote when key boundary skipped
workaround: index_of full prefix "\"verdict\":\"" then substring(pos+11, len), find next \" — len("\"verdict\":\"")=11 fixed offset

issue: R2(r) absolute scale depends on sample density (sedi N=141 vs eeg N=75 → R2 ~24 vs ~16)
workaround: normalize each R2 series to mean=1 before KS/L2 — Pearson is scale-invariant but absolute distances aren't

issue: combinatorial atlas sub-graph is a star forest / tree — Laplacian spectrum is degenerate integer-valued (multiplicities at λ=2,3,18,...) → pair correlation R₂(r) cannot reach Wigner GUE regardless of axis
workaround: to reach 0.7+ require either (a) much larger atlas with truly random-like topology (>10K nodes per sub-net), (b) spectral perturbation by random rewiring fraction r, or (c) abandon graph-Laplacian path and use field-theoretic spectrum (e.g., correlator of physics constants)

issue: proxy-data verdict (paper-sizes / learning_log timestamps, 2026-04-14) was not equivalent to real-source verdict — KS=1.78 → 45.4 (25× larger), composite path drift
workaround: extract real wow_signal.json extended_context (35 SNR ints) + real anima EEG protocol_log block-level metrics (alpha/gamma/theta/RT/accuracy, n=18). Proxy paths kept in commit history only.

issue: SETI 35-bin Wow! signal (mostly zeros + 6 nonzero peak) becomes 'mixed' verdict (0.501) but anima EEG (continuous psychometrics) is strongly 'Poisson' (0.086) — agreement=0.585 modest, cosine=0.175 very low
workaround: two domains have fundamentally different spectral character — single-spike radio transient vs continuous cognitive metric. Composite=0.438 < 0.7 BT — divergent, but data_missing=false (real-data verdict, statistically valid). To reach 0.7+: aggregate multi-event SETI catalog (Allen Telescope archive) vs multi-session EEG longitudinal, both as continuous time-series.
